meaning: n.调整者,校准者,调整器,标准仪
examples: An independent regulator will be appointed to ensure fair competition.
"Cayman takes advantage of a legitimate loophole—and the financial system is none the weaker for it," says one regulator in Europe.
The government, which has stepped back from its traditional role as a regulator of work time should take steps to make shorter hours possible.
Oftel, the telecoms regulator, says it cannot predict how demand in certain areas will change.
Railtrack reduced delays by only 10%, against a target of 12.7% set by the Regulator.
